nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or limited tool
nodes.grounding_span: I use it daily both as a collaborator itself and within other tools for productivity

nodes.label: AI as overenthusiastic intern requiring oversight
nodes.grounding_span: I mostly treat it like I would a law student intern I guess, get drafts and go back if big changes are needed but nothing is completely independent of my control

nodes.label: AI for client communication
nodes.grounding_span: I don't use the tools for any actual interactions with the clients where there's some chance it could go wrong, only to analyze and summarize them for later use

nodes.label: AI as tool for routine legal work
nodes.label_negative: AI's ability to handle rigid formatting
nodes.grounding_span: the way it approaches document creation by re-creating things over and over instead of editing what already exists is not how the legal system is setup with its rigid formatting rules

nodes.label: Embracing AI despite replacement concerns
nodes.grounding_span: I embrace it even if it kind of does feel like training my replacement at times

nodes.label: AI as practical problem solver
nodes.label_negative: AI taking over all legal work
nodes.grounding_span: it will help us specialize more in helping complicated cases while leaving the mundane phone calls giving the same advice over and over again eventually to AI

nodes.label: Professional responsibility and client safety
nodes.grounding_span: I would never sign my name as a lawyer to something I don't finish myself though

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: help keep me oriented as someone with ADHD still being able to carry an enormous load of cases

nodes.label: High-quality legal service and access to justice
nodes.grounding_span: allowing us to help so many more people and help the people who need it with higher quality service

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: it usually gets me pretty close within 1 or 2 revisions

nodes.label: Frustration with AI formatting
nodes.grounding_span: It makes me feel frustrated every time I see something that seems so simple which it can't do

nodes.label: Proactive adaptation to AI rather than fear of replacement
nodes.grounding_span: I embrace it even if it kind of does feel like training my replacement at times

nodes.label: Optimism about AI enabling specialization
nodes.grounding_span: it will help us specialize more in helping complicated cases

nodes.label: Strategic multi-tool orchestration — uses different AI models for different strengths
nodes.grounding_span: I do tend to use multiple tools for things though, for example asking westlaw co-counsel to find case law and arguments because that is trusted info and then using that to help claude which excels at the actual writing part

nodes.label: Boundary-setting and ethical deliberation
nodes.grounding_span: I don't use the tools for any actual interactions with the clients where there's some chance it could go wrong
